topic: Expressing Feelings with Figurative Language and Sun Mythology
title: Expressing Feelings with Figurative Language
simple_explanation: This page is like a drama lesson with words! We learn cool phrases for being super happy or super grumpy. Then, we use these phrases to make up our own rhyming poems and read them out loud, showing the feelings with our voices. We also learn about how people long, long ago thought the sun was a powerful god!
detailed_explanation: You'll practice reading these expressions aloud and sorting them. Then, you'll get to be creative! You can take these phrases and turn them into rhyming lines or even a short poem. Imagine making your own special poem about a happy or grumpy day. When you share your poem, remember to use your voice to show the feelings. If your poem talks about being 'on cloud nine,' make your voice sound happy and light! This makes your performance much more engaging for your listeners. Finally, we'll take a quick trip back in time! Did you know ancient people believed the sun had special powers and even named sun gods? You'll get to research some of these old myths and even try rewriting one as a poem, using personification to bring the sun character to life!

examples_expanded.text: Happy expression: 'I'm *on cloud nine* after getting full marks!'
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of expressing feelings with figurative language and sun mythology in a real sentence.

examples_expanded.text: Grumpy expression: 'My brother was *like a bear with a sore head* this morning.'
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of expressing feelings with figurative language and sun mythology in a real sentence.

examples_expanded.text: Poem line: 'I'll be *on a short fuse* if I hear more bad news!'
examples_expanded.context: This example shows how we use the concept of expressing feelings with figurative language and sun mythology in a real sentence.

guided_practice: Identify the meaning of 'figurative expressions' in a sentence. Remember it means: phrases that use figurative language to describe something, often feelings (e.g., 'over the moon').
Match the word 'on cloud nine' with a similar concept from your own experience.
Look at the first example again. How would you modify it to make it your own?
application_tasks: Create 3 of your own sentences using the core idea of Expressing Feelings with Figurative Language and Sun Mythology.
Imagine you are teaching this topic to a friend. Write down exactly what you would say.
challenge_tasks: Combine the concept of 'figurative expressions' with something you learned in a previous chapter to write a short paragraph.
Can you find an exception to this rule or a creative way to bend it? Explain how.
key_points: Recognize and use common figurative expressions for emotions.
Create short poems or rhyming lines using these expressions.
Perform poems expressively, using voice to convey mood.
Explore ancient myths about the sun and rewrite them using personification.

vocabulary.definition: phrases that use figurative language to describe something, often feelings (e.g., 'over the moon').

vocabulary.word: like a bear with a sore head

vocabulary.definition: an ancient story, often about gods or heroes, that explains beliefs or natural events.
